title: Joy Is Allowed to Be Medicine
subtitle: An affirmation for giving pleasure a role in how you rest and recover
excerpt: A short affirmation from Day 24 of a 30-day sleep program. The statement names joy and ease as active contributors to rest, not as distractions or luxuries, a reframe of the survival-mode habit of getting through the day without pleasure.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 24 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: A short affirmation that places joy and ease next to rest as active parts of recovery. The line moves pleasure out of the reward column and into the working part of the day. Not something earned after effort, but a steady part of how the body finds its way back to calm.
Joy is allowed to be medicine. Ease is medicine. I let small pleasures be part of how I rest and recover.
Say it three times, slow. If the mind produces a list of things to finish before you are allowed ease, that is the habit the affirmation is working against. Say it one more time.
Adapted from Day 24 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
